title: pouteria rigidifolia
intro: Pouteria rigidifolia, also known as the white wrightia, is a tropical plant species native to the Philippines and Malaysia, belonging to the family Sapotaceae. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine, where its bark and leaves are used to treat various health conditions. The bark is rich in flavonoids and alkaloids, which have been found to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, providing health benefits such as reduced fever, relief from respiratory issues, and improved digestion. Herbal preparations of Pouteria rigidifolia can be made into teas, infusions, or dried extracts, which are used to alleviate symptoms of coughs, colds, and other respiratory problems.
